---
title: "Additional figures screening"
author: "Miléna"
format: html
editor: visual
---

```{r}
targets::tar_load(compounds_samples_spagg_to_keep)
targets::tar_source(here::here("R/analysis_internship.R"))

data <- compounds_samples_spagg_to_keep |> dplyr::filter(!class %in% c( "Sesquiterpenes", "Oxygenated-sesquiterpenes")) |> 
  dplyr::select(-smiles, -class, -inchikey) |> dplyr::mutate(across(c(where(is.character), -compound), as.numeric))|> 
  tidyr::pivot_longer(cols = -compound,names_to = "sample",values_to = "value") |> 
  tidyr::pivot_wider( names_from = "compound", values_from = "value") |> 
  dplyr::mutate(across(-sample, ~ ifelse(is.na(.), 0, .))) |>  
  dplyr::mutate(gragg = stringr::str_to_upper(stringr::str_sub(sample, 1,4)))


sample_col <- data |> dplyr::select(sample, gragg)
numeric_cols <- data |> dplyr::select(-sample, -gragg)

# Normaliser les colonnes numériques
numeric_cols_normalised <- normaliser_dataframe(numeric_cols)

# Réassembler avec la colonne sample
data_N <- cbind(sample = sample_col, numeric_cols_normalised)

pca = princomp(data_N[,3 :32])
  
biplot(pca)

ggplot2::autoplot(
  pca,
  data = data_N,
  colour = "sample.gragg",      # Colorer par cette variable
  label = TRUE,
  label.label = "sample.gragg",  # Afficher ces labels
  label.size = 3,
  loadings = TRUE,
  loadings.label = TRUE,
  loadings.colour = "lightgray",        # Flèches en gris clair
  loadings.label.colour = "lightgray"   # Labels des flèches en gris clair
) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(colour = "Sample Group")  # Renommer la légende
```
